title: Plastic waste generation by region and polymer
subtitle: Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).
caption:
Plastic waste generation by region and polymer

Polymer types are as follows: Acrylonitrile Butadiene (AB), Acrylonitrile Styrene Acrylate (ASA), Styrene Acrylonitrile (SAN), Polyurethane (PUR), Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC), Polystyrene (PS), Linear Low-Density Polyethylene (LLDPE), Polypropylene (PP), Polyethylene Terephthalate (PET), High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE).

This stacked horizontal bar chart displays the regional composition of plastic waste generation for various polymer types in 2019, with regional aggregates calculated by Our World in Data based on OECD specifications. The vertical axis lists thirteen categories, including specific polymers like PVC, PUR, and PS, as well as grouped categories such as "LDPE, LLDPE" and "ABS, ASA, SAN." Each bar represents 100% of the waste for that category, segmented by color to show the share contributed by nine distinct regions: Europe, China, Asia (excluding China and India), Middle East & North Africa, India, Sub-Saharan Africa, Americas (excluding USA), Oceania, and the United States. Across nearly all polymer types, the United States, represented by the teal segment on the far right, consistently accounts for a substantial portion of the total, typically ranging between 15% and 25%. Europe (blue) and China (maroon) also appear as major contributors in most categories, frequently occupying the leftmost sections of the bars with shares often falling between 15% and 25% each. The "Road marking coatings" category presents a distinct distribution where the Americas (excluding USA), shown in pink, extends significantly further than in other rows, reaching approximately 60% of the total. Conversely, categories like "Fibres" and "Marine coatings" show a more fragmented distribution among the middle segments, including India, the Middle East & North Africa, and Asia (excluding China and India), which collectively occupy a larger share of the bar compared to other polymer types. The "Other" category follows a distribution pattern similar to the major polymers, with the United States, Europe, and China forming the largest individual blocks.
